title: Shell Wrapper: Agent Resolved from Session Evidence
description: Shell preexec captures 'cat ~/.aws/credentials'. The shell-wrapper provider emits actor_claim.kind='shell'. The orchestrator resolves the actor to ai_coding_agent by fusing the shell signal with active agent-session evidence (PID+start-time matches the agent session). With resolved_actor_kind=ai_coding_agent, the deny-agent-credential-read rule fires. This is the 'shell provider stays honest; kernel resolves with confidence' design.
note: actor_claim.kind='shell' — the shell provider is honest; it does not self-label as ai_coding_agent. The orchestrator sets resolved_actor_kind='ai_coding_agent' after matching pid=9001 to an active agent session. The deny-agent-credential-read rule fires on the resolved identity. Attribution confidence is medium (declared_intent). The shell provider's honesty is preserved; resolution happens in the orchestrator, not the provider.
